Prevention Services
Modoc Food Policy Council
Parents as Teachers -
this program is an Evidence-Based Home Visiting model that provides a wide array of services to families with children from prenatal through kindergarten ages and also offers deep insights into early childhood development.
Maternal, Child and Adolescent Health
Breastfeeding Support - Phone, office, or home visits with lactation educator. Pumps and supplies available as needed.
Perinatal Services - Pregnancy testing, prenatal and childbirth education, and post-partum support.
Injury Prevention - Car seat education, bike safety, pedestrian safety and other childhood safety concerns.
Adolescent Health - Assistance with accessing health care services and resources.
Healthy Aging
-
This program focuses on collaborative ways to help individuals gain the knowledge, tools, and
confidence necessary for successful self-management of chronic diseases and other health conditions.
We utilize an evidence-based program called EnhanceWellness as our guide. It features one-on-one health
coaching.
For more information, call Public Health at 530-233-6311.
